emails.body:
Jeff, James —

Thought I'd check in now that the fiscal year is winding down. Both of you are approaching those decision points where the next set of orders starts to shape the back half of your careers. Happy to talk through XO tour timing, shore duty options, or even the JCWS/JPME pipeline if that's on your radar.

No agenda, just an open line. Let me know what's on your mind.

Laura

CAPT Kim,

Appreciate the reach-out. I've been weighing whether to push for an afloat XO slot in the spring cycle or take the NPS quota I've got tentatively lined up for winter. The operational side pulls me, but the academic window doesn't stay open forever. Would value your read on how the board tends to view that trade-off right now.

Jeff

CAPT Kim,

Following up on Jeff's note — I'm in a similar spot but leaning toward the afloat XO route. My detailer has a couple of DDG slots projected for March/April, and I'm trying to get a sense of which hulls would set up a competitive CO screen later. Also curious if you're seeing any shift in how the board weights recent at-sea time versus staff tours.

James

Jeff, James —

Good discussion. A few thoughts: the board still treats a successful afloat XO tour as the gold standard for CO eligibility, no question. That said, NPS or a similar shore-based master's doesn't close the door — it just means you need a strong follow-on sea tour to stay competitive. I've seen both paths work.

The variable that doesn't show up on the fitrep is family stability. Spouse employment, kids' school cycles, elderly parents — those factors derail more careers than any board decision. If you've got a window where the family piece is settled, that's the time to take the harder sea duty. If not, the shore option buys you stability without killing your record.

Happy to walk through specific hulls or programs offline. Let me know.

Laura

CAPT Kim,

That's the calculus I needed spelled out. My wife's got a tenure-track review coming up next spring, so stability weighs heavy. I'll probably take the NPS slot and aim for a deep-draft XO tour after. Thanks for the straight talk.

Jeff
